exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter.
instructions: Setup: Load the bar and set safety pins or use a spotter. Lie supine on a flat bench with eyes under the bar, feet flat on the floor and a slight lumbar arch.
Grip: Use a close grip (hands about shoulder-width or slightly narrower — roughly 6–12 inches apart) with a pronated grip, wrists stacked over wrists and the bar.
Scapula and position: Retract and depress your shoulder blades slightly to create a stable chest platform; keep shoulders down away from ears.
Unrack: Drive your feet, take a deep breath, brace your core, lock your elbows to lift the bar out of the rack and position it over your sternum with arms vertical.
Descent: Lower the bar in a controlled manner to the lower chest/sternum while keeping your elbows tucked close to your torso (avoid wide flaring), inhaling as you descend.
Touch and pause: Lightly touch or stop just above the chest (avoid bouncing) and maintain tension in the triceps.
Press: Exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows, keeping the bar path slightly diagonal toward the head until near full extension (do not hyperextend or lock aggressively).
Re-rack: After the set, control the bar back to the rack hooks or have a spotter assist. Maintain form on every rep.

Coaching cues & safety: Use a spotter or safety pins for heavy sets, keep wrists neutral, avoid flaring elbows to protect shoulders, and reduce weight if you cannot maintain elbow tuck and controlled descent.
exercises.description: A supine barbell press using a narrow grip that shifts emphasis from the chest to the triceps. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the lower chest/sternum with elbows kept close to the torso and then pressed back to lockout.

exercises.name: EZ barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench and load an EZ bar with appropriate weight; position a spotter if using heavy loads.
instructions: Set up a flat bench and load an EZ bar with appropriate weight; position a spotter if using heavy loads.
Lie supine on the bench with your eyes roughly under the bar, feet flat on the floor and knees bent to create a stable base.
Grasp the EZ bar with a narrow/close grip (hands placed inside the outer bends of the EZ bar), wrists straight and palms facing up/neutral.
Unrack the bar and hold it directly over your chest with arms extended, shoulders retracted slightly and core braced.
Slowly lower the bar in a controlled path to the middle of your chest/nipple line while keeping your elbows close to your torso (not flared wide).
Pause briefly at the bottom without bouncing, then drive the bar upward by extending the elbows until your arms are nearly straight but not fully hyperextended.
Exhale as you press up, inhale as you lower. Maintain wrist alignment and a tight torso throughout the set.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, and carefully rack the bar with control at the end of the set.
exercises.description: Lying supine on a flat bench, perform a close-grip press with an EZ bar to emphasize elbow extension and overload the triceps while still engaging the chest and front delts. Use a narrow grip and keep the elbows tucked to maximize triceps activation.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
Hold the barbell with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip so your palms face forward and the bar rests on the base of the fingers.
Start with your arms fully extended down at your thighs, elbows tucked close to your torso and wrists neutral (no excessive bending).
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing the elbows, keeping the upper arms and elbows stationary at your sides — lift until the forearms approach the biceps or the bar is near chest level.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the biceps, avoiding shoulder shrugging or leaning back (no trunk momentum).
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start position with a controlled eccentric motion until the arms are fully extended.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining steady tempo and strict form; reduce weight if you cannot keep elbows stable and avoid swinging the torso.
exercises.description: Standing barbell curl performed with a shoulder-width, underhand (supinated) grip; the exercise isolates elbow flexion to work the biceps with the forearms and delts assisting for stabilization. Use a controlled concentric lift and slow eccentric lowering for full muscle stimulation.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.description: A supine barbell press using a narrow grip that shifts emphasis from the chest to the triceps. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the lower chest/sternum with elbows kept close to the torso and then pressed back to lockout.

exercises.name: Cable Pushdown
exercises.notes: Set a high pulley and attach a rope (or straight bar).
instructions: Set a high pulley and attach a rope (or straight bar). Select an appropriate weight that allows controlled movement.
Stand facing the machine with feet hip-width apart, knees slightly bent, and a slight forward lean from the hips; brace your core.
Grasp the rope with a neutral (palms facing) grip and bring your elbows to your sides so your upper arms are vertical and stationary.
Exhale and press the rope down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and the rope ends are slightly spread apart at the bottom.
Pause briefly at full extension while keeping shoulders down and upper arms fixed; avoid using torso swing or stepping for momentum.
Inhale and slowly allow the cable to raise your hands back to the start position, controlling the eccentric phase until your forearms return to vertical.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining tucked elbows and a stable torso throughout the set.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdowns (rope attachment) target the triceps by extending the elbow against resistance while the upper arms remain stationary. The movement also engages the forearms for grip and the delts and abs for posture and stabilization.

exercises.name: EZ Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
instructions: Stand upright (standing) with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Grip the EZ bar on the angled (inner) hand positions so your palms are facing upward/neutral; hands about shoulder-width apart.
Keep your chest up, shoulders back, and core braced to stabilize the spine; elbows should sit close to your sides.
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing your elbows, driving the movement with your biceps; keep your upper arms stationary and forearms moving.
Pause briefly at the top when the bar is near shoulder level and biceps are fully contracted; avoid shrugging or swinging the torso.
Inhale and lower the bar slowly and under control back to full elbow extension without locking the joints.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo and strict form throughout.
exercises.description: Standing curl using an EZ bar with a semi-supinated grip to isolate elbow flexion and develop the biceps. Perform controlled concentric and eccentric reps while keeping the torso still to minimize cheating.

exercises.name: Barbell Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Reach forward and grasp the barbell with an underhand (supinated) grip about shoulder-width apart, letting your arms fully extend so your elbows are slightly bent and resting on the pad.
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with your chest and front of your upper arms pressed firmly against the angled pad and feet flat on the floor (seated position).
Reach forward and grasp the barbell with an underhand (supinated) grip about shoulder-width apart, letting your arms fully extend so your elbows are slightly bent and resting on the pad.
Brace your core, keep your torso still and shoulders relaxed (no shrugging), and ensure your wrists remain neutral throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl the barbell upward by flexing the elbows, driving the movement with the biceps; keep the upper arms locked to the pad so only the forearms move.
Pause briefly at the top when the bar is near shoulder level and you feel maximal biceps contraction.
Inhale and slowly lower the barbell back to the start position with controlled tension until your arms are near full extension but not locked out.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining strict form (no swinging or lifting the torso) and using a weight that allows full control.
If wrists or elbows feel strained, reduce weight or use an EZ bar to improve wrist comfort; stop if you feel sharp pain.
exercises.description: Seated barbell preacher curl performed on an angled preacher bench to isolate the biceps by preventing shoulder movement; the upper arms remain supported on the pad while you curl the barbell. Focus is on controlled concentric and eccentric motion to maximize biceps activation.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Standing Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly soft; brace your abs and maintain a neutral spine.
instructions: Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and knees slightly soft; brace your abs and maintain a neutral spine.
Grasp a single dumbbell with both hands by the handle or top plate and lift it overhead so the weight is directly above your head with arms fully extended.
Keep your upper arms (elbows) pointing up and close to your head; avoid letting elbows flare out to the sides.
Inhale and slowly bend your elbows to lower the dumbbell behind your head until your forearms are roughly parallel to the floor or you feel a comfortable stretch in the triceps.
Pause briefly at the bottom without moving your upper arms, then exhale and extend the elbows to raise the dumbbell back to the starting overhead position using the triceps.
Keep wrists neutral and upper arms stationary throughout; do not arch the lower back—maintain core tension to prevent torso movement.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, selecting a weight that allows controlled movement and full elbow extension without momentum.
exercises.description: Standing overhead dumbbell triceps extension performed with the dumbbell held behind the head; the elbows flex to lower the weight and then extend to drive the weight back overhead, isolating the triceps. Performed standing with core braced to stabilize the torso.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Kickback
exercises.notes: Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor, maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
instructions: Position yourself with one knee and the same-side hand resting on a flat bench (all fours variation with one leg off the bench); place the opposite foot on the floor for stability.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor, maintain a neutral spine and brace your abs.
Hold a dumbbell in the free hand with a neutral grip (palm facing your torso) and let your upper arm hang so the elbow is bent about 90° and tucked close to your side.
Keep the upper arm stationary and exhale as you extend your elbow, driving the dumbbell straight back until your arm is fully extended and the triceps is contracted.
Pause briefly at full extension, focusing on squeezing the triceps, then inhale and slowly return the dumbbell to the starting 90° elbow position with control.
Perform the desired repetitions, then switch sides. Avoid swinging your torso or using momentum — only the forearm should move.
exercises.description: Single-arm dumbbell kickback performed with one hand and knee supported on a bench; extend the elbow to drive the dumbbell back and straighten the arm, emphasizing the triceps. Keep the torso stable and hinge at the hips so only the forearm moves.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Close Grip Bench Press
exercises.notes: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
instructions: Set up a flat bench in a rack or on a bench press station and load the barbell safely; position the bar at arm level for easy unracking.
Lie supine (on your back) on the bench with feet flat on the floor, knees bent, shoulders retracted and pressed into the bench for stability.
Grip the barbell with a close grip: hands about shoulder-width apart (index fingers roughly in line with the shoulders) or slightly narrower; keep wrists neutral and stacked over elbows.
Unrack the bar by straightening your arms and moving the bar directly above your sternum; keep a tight core and stable foot drive.
Inhale and lower the bar under control to the lower chest / sternum area, keeping elbows tucked closer to your body (about 45 degrees or less from the torso) to emphasize the triceps.
Pause briefly with the bar lightly touching the chest (do not bounce), then exhale and press the bar upward by extending the elbows until arms are nearly straight but not hyperextended.
Maintain wrist alignment and shoulder position throughout; avoid flaring the elbows wide and keep the movement vertical over the chest.
Rerack the bar with control after the set; use a spotter when lifting heavy or if you are near failure.
Progress by increasing load gradually, or modify grip width slightly to change emphasis; stop or reduce range if you feel joint pain (especially in wrists or elbows).
exercises.description: A compound supine pressing exercise performed with a narrow (close) grip to place primary emphasis on the triceps while still recruiting the pectorals and anterior delts. Performed on a flat bench, the bar is lowered to the chest and pressed back to full elbow extension.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lying Alternate Extension
exercises.notes: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and core braced.
instructions: Lie supine on a flat bench with feet flat on the floor and core braced.
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with arms extended straight above your chest and wrists neutral (palms facing each other or facing forward depending on preference).
Keep your upper arms stationary and elbows pointed toward the ceiling (avoid letting elbows flare out).
Lower one dumbbell by flexing only the elbow, bringing the weight toward your forehead or just behind the head in a controlled motion.
Pause briefly at the bottom, then extend the elbow to straighten the arm and return the dumbbell to the start position while keeping the other arm still.
Alternate and repeat with the opposite arm, maintaining a slow, controlled tempo and steady breathing (exhale as you extend, inhale as you lower).
Keep the shoulder and upper arm stable throughout; do not use momentum or arch the lower back to lift the weight.
exercises.description: A supine single- and alternating-arm dumbbell triceps extension that isolates the triceps by bending and extending the elbow while the upper arm remains stationary. Performed lying on a bench, each arm is lowered and extended alternately to work the long, lateral and medial heads of the triceps.

exercises.name: Cable Overhead Triceps Extension Straight Bar
exercises.notes: Step 1: Attach a straight bar to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Step 1: Attach a straight bar to the high pulley and select an appropriate weight.
Step 2: Stand facing away from the machine with feet staggered (one foot slightly forward for balance) and grasp the bar with a shoulder-width, overhand grip.

Step 3: Raise the bar overhead and bend the elbows so the bar is positioned behind your head; keep upper arms close to your ears and elbows pointing upward (slight forward lean at the hips is acceptable).
Step 4: Brace your abs, keep a neutral spine, and stabilize your shoulders (minimize movement of the delts).
Step 5: Exhale and extend both elbows to press the bar upward until your arms are fully straight but not locked aggressively; squeeze the triceps at the top.
Step 6: Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start position behind your head with controlled tension (controlled eccentric), keeping the upper arms stationary.
Step 7: Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining strict form; avoid flaring elbows out, arching the lower back, or using momentum from the shoulders.
Step 8: To finish safely, return the bar to the starting support or lower the weight before releasing your grip.
exercises.description: Standing cable overhead triceps extension using a straight bar: perform controlled elbow extensions with the bar starting behind the head and finishing with arms straight overhead to isolate the triceps. Maintain a stable torso and stationary upper arms so the movement occurs at the elbows.

exercises.name: Cable Triceps Pushdown (SZ bar)
exercises.notes: Attach an SZ (curl) bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach an SZ (curl) bar to the high pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent and core braced (upright torso).
Grab the SZ bar with a neutral/overhand grip (palms facing down or slightly toward you), hands about shoulder-width apart.
Start with the bar at chest/upper-ab level, elbows tucked close to your sides and upper arms stationary (forearms up).
Exhale and press the bar down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight and triceps are contracted; keep wrists neutral and avoid shrugging or leaning forward.
Pause briefly at full extension, then inhale and slowly allow the bar to rise back to the starting position by bending the elbows while maintaining upper-arm position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while keeping movement controlled and avoiding momentum or excessive torso movement.
exercises.description: Standing cable pushdown using an SZ (curl) bar that emphasizes elbow extension to work the triceps; performed facing the cable machine with the upper arms held stationary at the sides. Keep a neutral spine and controlled tempo, extending the arms fully and returning under control.

exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.notes: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
instructions: Stand upright with feet about shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and core braced (standing position).
Hold the barbell with a shoulder-width underhand (supinated) grip so your palms face forward and the bar rests on the base of the fingers.
Start with your arms fully extended down at your thighs, elbows tucked close to your torso and wrists neutral (no excessive bending).
Exhale and curl the bar upward by flexing the elbows, keeping the upper arms and elbows stationary at your sides — lift until the forearms approach the biceps or the bar is near chest level.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the biceps, avoiding shoulder shrugging or leaning back (no trunk momentum).
Inhale and slowly lower the bar back to the start position with a controlled eccentric motion until the arms are fully extended.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining steady tempo and strict form; reduce weight if you cannot keep elbows stable and avoid swinging the torso.
exercises.description: Standing barbell curl performed with a shoulder-width, underhand (supinated) grip; the exercise isolates elbow flexion to work the biceps with the forearms and delts assisting for stabilization. Use a controlled concentric lift and slow eccentric lowering for full muscle stimulation.

exercises.name: Cable Hammer Curl (with rope)
exercises.notes: Attach a rope handle to the low pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Attach a rope handle to the low pulley of a cable machine and select an appropriate weight.
Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart, knees soft, and the pulley between your legs; hold the rope ends with a neutral grip (thumbs-up) so palms face each other.
Start with your arms fully extended and the rope near mid-thigh; keep your torso upright and shoulders down and back.
Keeping your elbows pinned to your sides, exhale and curl the rope upward by flexing at the elbow, bringing your hands toward your shoulders while maintaining the neutral (thumbs-up) wrist position.
At the top of the movement, pause briefly and squeeze the biceps/forearms without swinging or leaning back.
Inhale and slowly lower the rope back to the start under control until your arms are fully extended.
Repeat for the desired number of reps, maintaining strict elbow position and avoiding momentum throughout the set.
exercises.description: Standing cable hammer curl with a rope is a neutral-grip curling variation that places constant tension on the biceps while strongly engaging the forearm (brachioradialis) muscles. The rope allows a thumbs-up position throughout the movement for a hammer-style curl.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Preacher Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the preacher bench seat so when seated your upper arms (from armpit to elbow) rest flat and supported on the angled pad and your feet are flat on the floor.
instructions: Adjust the preacher bench seat so when seated your upper arms (from armpit to elbow) rest flat and supported on the angled pad and your feet are flat on the floor.

Sit upright on the bench, lean slightly forward, and pick up a dumbbell in each hand with a supinated (underhand) grip; let your arms hang straight down with elbows resting on the pad and a slight bend in the elbow.
Brace your core, keep your chest against the pad, and keep your upper arms pinned to the pad throughout the movement (do not let the elbows travel forward or the torso rock).
Exhale and curl the dumbbells by flexing the elbows only, bringing the weights toward your shoulders while squeezing the biceps at the top; maintain neutral wrists (do not curl with the wrist).

Pause briefly at the top for a controlled contraction, then inhale and slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position until the arms are near full extension while still keeping the upper arms supported on the pad.
Repeat for the desired reps using controlled tempo; avoid using momentum or swinging the torso and do not hyperextend the elbows at the bottom.
exercises.description: Seated preacher dumbbell curls isolate the biceps by stabilizing the upper arm on an angled preacher pad, allowing strict elbow flexion. Performed seated with the arms supported, they emphasize peak contraction and slow negatives for the biceps.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Concentration Curl
exercises.notes: Pick up a dumbbell with one hand using a fully supinated (palm-up) grip and let your arm hang between your legs.
instructions: Sit on the end of a flat bench with feet flat on the floor about hip-width apart and knees bent.
Pick up a dumbbell with one hand using a fully supinated (palm-up) grip and let your arm hang between your legs.
Lean forward slightly at the hips and place the back of your upper arm (triceps area) or elbow against the inside of the same-side thigh so the elbow is fixed.
Brace your core and keep your upper arm stationary; this is a seated, supported position to isolate the biceps.
Exhale and curl the dumbbell upward by flexing the elbow until the dumbbell approaches shoulder level, focusing on contracting the biceps and keeping the wrist neutral.
Pause and squeeze the biceps at the top for 1 second without shrugging the shoulder or rocking the torso.
Inhale and slowly lower the dumbbell back to the fully extended starting position with control (eccentric phase).
Avoid using body momentum or moving the upper arm; switch sides after completing the set for the opposite arm.
exercises.description: Seated, single-arm dumbbell curl performed with the working elbow braced against the inside of the same-side thigh to isolate the biceps. The exercise emphasizes a strict concentric curl and controlled eccentric lowering to maximize biceps contraction.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Squat
exercises.notes: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height.
instructions: Set the barbell in a rack at about shoulder height. Step under the bar and position it across the front of your shoulders (front of delts/upper chest) using either a clean (front-rack) grip or a cross-arm hold.
Stand up to unrack the bar, take one or two small steps back, and place your feet about shoulder-width apart with toes pointed slightly out.
Raise your elbows so your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor and brace your abs to keep the torso upright throughout the movement.
Inhale, push your hips back slightly and bend at the knees to descend into a squat; keep your chest up, elbows high, and knees tracking over your toes.
Continue lowering until your thighs reach at least parallel to the floor (or slightly below if mobility allows) while maintaining a neutral spine.
Exhale and drive through your heels and midfoot to extend your knees and hips, returning to the standing start position with control.
Maintain tight abs and high elbows on every rep; avoid letting the knees cave inward or the torso fold forward. Rack the bar safely when finished.
exercises.description: A front-loaded barbell squat where the barbell is held across the front of the shoulders, emphasizing upright torso and knee extension to target the quadriceps. Performed by descending into a squat and driving back up while keeping elbows high and core braced.

exercises.name: Barbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart and the barbell over the middle of your feet; grip the bar just outside your thighs with an overhand or mixed grip.
Brace your core, pull your shoulders back slightly, and set a neutral spine; keep a small, fixed bend in the knees (soft knees) — do not squat.
Initiate the movement by pushing your hips straight back (hip hinge) and allow the bar to descend close to your legs, keeping your chest up and eyes forward.
Continue hinging until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings (bar typically reaches just below the knees or mid-shin depending on flexibility) while maintaining a flat back.
Pause briefly at the bottom without rounding the lower back; keep the lats engaged to keep the bar close to the body.
Drive the hips forward by contracting the glutes and hamstrings to return to a standing position, finishing with hips fully extended but without hyperextending the spine.
Breathe: inhale on the descent, exhale as you drive the hips forward. Repeat for desired repetitions, focusing on controlled tempo and maintaining the hip-hinge pattern.
exercises.description: A hip-hinge movement performed standing with a barbell that emphasizes eccentric and concentric loading of the hamstrings and glutes while the posterior chain and grip stabilize the load. Maintain a neutral spine and a soft knee angle throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Barbell Floor Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Load a barbell and position it across the upper back/trapezius, not the neck; grip the bar just outside shoulder width with a firm hold.
instructions: Load a barbell and position it across the upper back/trapezius, not the neck; grip the bar just outside shoulder width with a firm hold.
Stand upright with feet about hip-width apart and weight evenly distributed on the mid-foot; keep a slight bend in the knees (not locked).
Brace your core and maintain an upright torso; focus on keeping shoulders down and the bar stable on your traps/delts.
Slowly raise both heels by pushing through the balls of your feet until you reach full plantarflexion (stand on your toes); contract the calves at the top and hold for 1–2 seconds.
Lower your heels back to the floor in a controlled manner until the soles are flat, feeling a stretch in the calves.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, breathing out on the ascent and inhaling on the descent.
If balance is an issue, perform the movement near a wall or use a spotter; avoid bouncing or using excessive forward trunk lean to lift the weight.
exercises.description: Standing barbell calf raise performed with a barbell resting on the upper back to load the lower legs; raise the heels by plantarflexing the ankles, pause at the top, then lower with control. This exercise primarily isolates the gastrocnemius/soleus (calves) while the upper back and shoulders stabilize the load.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at about mid-chest height. Position yourself under the bar so it rests across your upper traps (high-bar) or rear delts (low-bar) depending on preference.
Grip the bar with hands just outside shoulder width, elbows pointing down and chest up. Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core and take a full breath in, then unrack the bar by extending your legs and taking 1–2 controlled steps backward to clear the rack; establish a stable, shoulder-width stance.
Keep a neutral spine and an upright chest. Initiate the movement by pushing your hips back slightly and bending at the knees, sending your knees outward to track over your toes.
Descend in a controlled manner until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or deeper if mobility allows) while keeping weight on your midfoot/heels and maintaining a braced core.
Avoid knee collapse (valgus) — consciously push knees outward and keep toes engaged. Do not let the lower back round; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Drive through your heels and midfoot to stand back up, exhaling as you ascend and extending hips and knees simultaneously until fully upright.
Reset your breathing and bracing between reps as needed. When finished, step forward under control and re-rack the bar securely.
Use appropriate weight, a spotter or safety pins in the rack if lifting near maximum loads, and adjust stance or bar position to reduce discomfort in shoulders or lower back.
exercises.description: Barbell back squat is a compound, standing lower-body exercise that primarily develops the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes, hamstrings and core for stability. The barbell rests across the upper back while you descend by bending hips and knees, then drive back up to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Rack Lunge
exercises.notes: Setup: Place the barbell in a front rack position across the front of your shoulders (bar resting on anterior delts), grasping the bar with hands just outside shoulder width and elbows high; stand tall with feet hip-width apart.
instructions: Setup: Place the barbell in a front rack position across the front of your shoulders (bar resting on anterior delts), grasping the bar with hands just outside shoulder width and elbows high; stand tall with feet hip-width apart.
Step: Take a controlled step forward with one leg into a split stance (about 2–3 feet depending on height) so you are balanced and stable before descending.

Descend: Bend both knees and lower your body straight down until the front thigh is roughly parallel to the floor and the back knee is close to the ground (approximately 90° at both knees). Keep the torso upright, chest up, core braced and elbows high to maintain the front rack.

Knee and foot position: Keep the front knee tracking over the toes (avoid collapsing inward) and drive weight through the front heel as you descend and rise; the back foot remains on the ball of the foot.
Ascent: Push through the front heel to extend the front knee and hip and return to the standing start position in a controlled motion.
Repetition: Repeat for the prescribed number of reps on one leg or alternate legs each rep; maintain steady tempo, controlled range of motion and steady breathing (inhale down, exhale up).

Safety notes: Use a weight that allows you to keep elbows high and torso upright; avoid letting the front knee collapse medially or travel excessively far past the toes; if front-rack mobility is limited, use a clean grip or crossed-arm position.
exercises.description: Standing barbell lunge performed from a front-rack position; stepping forward and lowering the body emphasizes the quadriceps while the glutes, hamstrings and core stabilize. Maintain an upright torso with elbows high to support the bar on the front shoulders.

exercises.name: Smith Single Leg Deadlift
exercises.notes: Set the Smith machine bar at about mid-thigh height and load an appropriate weight; stand facing the machine and grasp the bar with a double overhand grip shoulder-width apart.
instructions: Set the Smith machine bar at about mid-thigh height and load an appropriate weight; stand facing the machine and grasp the bar with a double overhand grip shoulder-width apart.

Unrack the bar (or release the safety) and step back so you are standing on your working leg only; the non-working leg is lifted straight behind you (single-leg stance) with a slight bend in the standing knee.
Keep your chest up and spine neutral, brace your core, and initiate the movement by pushing your hips back (hip hinge) while keeping the back flat — do not round the spine.

Lower the bar in a vertical line close to the standing leg, sliding it down toward mid-shin while the rear leg rises to keep your torso and lifted leg roughly parallel to the floor; stop when you feel a strong but controlled stretch in the glutes/hamstrings or when your torso is nearly parallel to the floor.

Pause briefly at the bottom, then drive through the heel of the standing foot and contract the glute of the working leg to return the torso to upright and lower the rear leg back to the starting position.

Perform the prescribed reps on one leg, re-rack the bar (or set the safety) between sets if needed, then switch legs and repeat. Maintain controlled tempo and avoid using momentum; keep the knee of the working leg softly bent throughout.
exercises.description: A single-leg hip-hinge performed with the bar on a Smith machine that emphasizes unilateral glute and hamstring strength and balance while minimizing lateral bar path. The exercise requires a neutral spine and single-leg balance as you hinge forward and drive the hip back.

exercises.name: Sled Calf Press On Leg Press
exercises.notes: Load an appropriate weight on the leg press sled and sit/lie back with your back and head supported by the pad.
instructions: Load an appropriate weight on the leg press sled and sit/lie back with your back and head supported by the pad.
Place the balls of both feet on the lower edge of the footplate with heels hanging off; feet hip-width and toes pointing forward (modify toe angle to shift emphasis).
Unlock the safety/holders and extend the knees slightly but avoid locking them; keep hips and knees stable throughout the set.
Begin with the heels dropped to feel a stretch in the calves (ankle dorsiflexion) — this is the start position.
Exhale and press through the balls of your feet to plantarflex the ankles, raising the platform by contracting the calves until you reach a strong contraction (full plantarflexion).
Pause briefly and squeeze the calves at the top, then inhale and slowly lower the heels back down under control to the stretched start position.
Perform the desired number of repetitions, avoid bouncing at the bottom, maintain stable knees and torso, and re-engage the safety before exiting the machine.
exercises.description: Seated/lying calf raise performed using the leg press sled where the balls of the feet press the platform to plantarflex the ankle, isolating the calf muscles through a full range of motion.

exercises.name: Lever One Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.
Sit upright with your non-working leg resting relaxed, grip the side handles for stability, and brace your core.
Start with the working knee bent at about 90 degrees and the foot under the pad.
Exhale and extend the working leg by straightening the knee until it is nearly locked out but avoid full hyperextension; pause 1–2 seconds at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with controlled tension, keeping the movement smooth.
Complete the desired reps on one leg, then switch to the other leg; maintain an upright posture and avoid using momentum or swinging the torso.
exercises.description: A single-leg seated lever extension that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance on a leg extension machine. Performed one leg at a time to improve quad strength and muscular balance.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
instructions: Set the lever machine so the pivot aligns with the knee joint and place the lower-leg pad just above the heels / over the Achilles area.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with your hips flat on the pad, chest supported, and hands holding the front handles or bench for stability.
Position your legs fully extended with the pad resting on the back of your lower legs and feet relaxed; maintain a neutral spine throughout.
Exhale and curl your lower legs by flexing the knees, bringing your heels toward your glutes in a controlled motion until you reach near-full knee flexion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top for 1 second, keeping hips and torso pressed into the bench (avoid lifting the hips).
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start (near full knee extension) under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining steady tempo and full range of motion; adjust load so you can control both concentric and eccentric phases.
Stop or reduce weight if you feel excessive hip rise, lower-back strain, or knee pain; keep movements smooth and joint-focused.
exercises.description: Prone (lying face down) leg curl performed on a lever machine that isolates the hamstrings by flexing the knee against a padded lever. The movement curls the lower leg toward the buttocks while the torso remains stabilized on the bench.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.

exercises.name: Barbell Low Bar Squat
exercises.notes: Set the bar in a squat rack at mid-back height.
instructions: Set the bar in a squat rack at mid-back height. Step under the bar and place it across the rear delts/upper trapezius area just below the top of the shoulders (low-bar position), not on the neck.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width, retract the shoulder blades and squeeze the upper back to create a stable shelf; elbows should be pointed slightly down and back to support the bar.
Unrack the bar by straightening the legs and take 1–2 small steps back to clear the rack. Stand with feet about shoulder-width apart or slightly wider, toes pointed slightly outward.
Brace your core (tighten abs and breathe into the belly) and maintain a neutral spine; keep chest up and eyes forward. The torso will lean forward more than a high-bar squat but should remain rigid.
Begin the descent by pushing the hips back and bending the knees simultaneously; keep the knees tracking over the toes and push them outward to avoid caving in.
Lower until the thighs are at least parallel to the floor (or to your comfortable depth while maintaining form), keeping weight distributed through the midfoot and heels.
Drive upward by pushing through the heels and extending the hips and knees simultaneously while maintaining a braced core and tight upper back; exhale as you rise.
Return to a fully upright standing position with hips locked out, then repeat for the desired reps. Control the bar on each rep and reset your breath and brace between reps if needed.
When finished, walk the bar forward and carefully re-rack it on the squat rack. Use a spotter or safety pins if lifting heavy or training near failure.
exercises.description: A loaded barbell squat with the bar positioned low on the rear shoulders (across the posterior deltoids), emphasizing the thighs and posterior chain. The movement consists of a controlled hip-back-and-down descent to roughly parallel followed by an explosive drive back to standing.

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips and push them back while keeping a slight bend in the knees; reach down and grasp the bar with hands just outside the knees (mixed or double overhand grip).
instructions: Stand with feet about hip-width apart, toes pointing forward or slightly out, barbell over mid-foot.
Hinge at the hips and push them back while keeping a slight bend in the knees; reach down and grasp the bar with hands just outside the knees (mixed or double overhand grip).
Set a neutral spine (no rounding), pull shoulder blades down and back, lift the chest and brace the abs tightly.
Take a deep breath, drive through the heels and mid-foot, extend the hips and knees together to lift the bar, keeping the bar close to the shins and thighs.
Continue driving the hips forward until standing tall with hips fully extended and shoulders stacked over hips (lockout); do not hyperextend the lower back.
Pause briefly at the top with glutes engaged and core braced.
To lower, hinge at the hips first by pushing them back while maintaining a neutral spine, let the bar pass the knees, then bend the knees and place the bar back on the floor under control.
Reset breathing and core tension before each repetition and keep consistent form; avoid rounding the spine and jerking the bar.
exercises.description: A heavy compound hip-hinge lift that develops the glutes and the posterior chain by lifting a loaded barbell from the floor to a standing position. The movement also requires strong spinal bracing and upper-back stability while challenging grip strength.

exercises.name: Lever Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the knee joint lines up with the lever pivot and place the padded roller just above the ankles; select an appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the knee joint lines up with the lever pivot and place the padded roller just above the ankles; select an appropriate weight.
Sit upright with your back flat against the backrest, feet under the roller pad and hands holding the side handles or seat edges for stability.
Start with knees bent at about 90 degrees (or slightly less) and toes pointing forward; brace your core to keep the torso still.
Exhale and extend your knees by contracting the quads, pushing the roller pad upward until your legs are nearly straight — do not lock the knees.
Pause briefly at the top to feel the contraction in the quads.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight with control back to the starting knee angle, keeping tension in the quads throughout.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions. Avoid using momentum, excessive trunk lean, or hyperextending the knees.
exercises.description: Seated leg extension on a lever/machine that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knee against a padded roller. The movement is performed from a seated, back-supported position and focuses on controlled knee extension and return.

exercises.name: Lever One Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.
instructions: Adjust the machine seat so your back is flat against the backrest and your knee aligns with the machine's pivot point; place the lower shin pad just above the ankle of the working leg.
Sit upright with your non-working leg resting relaxed, grip the side handles for stability, and brace your core.
Start with the working knee bent at about 90 degrees and the foot under the pad.
Exhale and extend the working leg by straightening the knee until it is nearly locked out but avoid full hyperextension; pause 1–2 seconds at the top.
Inhale and slowly lower the weight back to the start position with controlled tension, keeping the movement smooth.
Complete the desired reps on one leg, then switch to the other leg; maintain an upright posture and avoid using momentum or swinging the torso.
exercises.description: A single-leg seated lever extension that isolates the quadriceps by extending the knee against resistance on a leg extension machine. Performed one leg at a time to improve quad strength and muscular balance.

exercises.name: Lever Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Adjust the machine so the hip support pad allows your hips to stay flat on the bench and the lever pivot aligns with your knees; select appropriate weight.
instructions: Adjust the machine so the hip support pad allows your hips to stay flat on the bench and the lever pivot aligns with your knees; select appropriate weight.
Lie prone (face down) on the bench with your knees just off the edge, chest supported, and hook your lower calves/ankles under the padded lever.
Grip the handles or edge of the bench for stability and keep your hips pressed into the bench throughout the movement.
Exhale and curl your heels up toward your glutes by flexing the knees until you reach a full but comfortable contraction.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze the hamstrings and glutes.
Inhale and slowly lower the lever back to the start with controlled extension of the knees, avoiding letting the hips rise.
Perform repetitions with steady tempo, focusing on full range of motion and control rather than momentum.
exercises.description: Prone (face down) leg curl performed on a lever machine that isolates the hamstrings by bending the knees to lift a pad toward the glutes. The movement emphasizes knee flexion with a controlled eccentric return.

exercises.name: Lever Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
instructions: Sit upright on the seated leg curl machine with your back firmly against the backrest and feet positioned under the lower padded roller just above the heels/ankles.
Adjust the thigh/pelvic pad so it rests snugly on top of your thighs to prevent your hips from rising during the movement; grip the handles for stability.
Start with knees nearly extended but not fully locked, torso upright and core engaged to stabilize the spine.
Exhale and curl the lower leg by flexing the knees, driving the padded roller down and back toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Pause and squeeze the hamstrings at the top of the movement without lifting your hips off the seat.
Inhale and slowly return to the start position, fully extending the knees under control without letting the weight slam or locking the knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions while keeping the torso still and avoiding swinging or using momentum.
exercises.description: Seated leg curl on a lever machine isolates knee flexion to train the hamstrings, with assistance from the glutes and calves. Performed seated with a padded lever that is pulled by bending the knees and bringing the heels toward the buttocks.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Seated Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Sit upright on a flat bench with knees bent at about 90 degrees and feet hip-width apart on an elevated surface (plate or block) so heels can drop below the toes.
instructions: Sit upright on a flat bench with knees bent at about 90 degrees and feet hip-width apart on an elevated surface (plate or block) so heels can drop below the toes.
Place one or two dumbbells vertically across your thighs just above the knees and hold them steady with your hands to keep them from rolling.
Keep your torso upright, core braced, and knees stationary throughout the movement.
Start with heels lowered to feel a stretch in the calves (ankles dorsiflexed).
Push through the balls of both feet and raise your heels as high as possible (plantarflexion) to fully contract the calves; hold for 1 second at the top.
Slowly lower the heels back down below the level of the elevated platform until you feel a controlled stretch; avoid bouncing.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining full range of motion and controlled tempo.
exercises.description: Seated calf raise performed with dumbbells places load on the soles of the feet while seated, emphasizing the calf muscles (especially the soleus). The movement raises and lowers the heels through plantarflexion while knees remain bent.

exercises.name: Cable Seated Crunch
exercises.notes: Attach a rope handle to a high pulley and sit upright on a bench facing away from the machine with feet flat on the floor and knees bent.
instructions: Attach a rope handle to a high pulley and sit upright on a bench facing away from the machine with feet flat on the floor and knees bent.
Grasp the rope near your head with both hands (hands beside your ears), keeping elbows bent and shoulders relaxed.
Brace your core and maintain a neutral pelvis; avoid thrusting the hips forward — hips should remain stationary on the bench.
Exhale and contract the abs to curl the torso forward and down, pulling the rope toward your thighs by flexing the spine (aim to bring your chest toward your knees).
Pause and squeeze the abdominal muscles at the bottom of the movement for 1 second.
Inhale and slowly reverse the motion, returning to the upright starting position with a controlled eccentric phase; do not let the weight pull you rapidly back.
Avoid pulling with the arms or using momentum; keep the movement driven by the abdominal contraction and maintain a steady tempo.
exercises.description: Seated cable crunch is a trunk‑flexion movement performed from a seated position using a high pulley and rope attachment to load the abdominal muscles. It emphasizes controlled spinal flexion to shorten the rectus abdominis while the upper body is pulled down toward the hips.

tips: Control the eccentric (lowering) phase for 2-3 seconds.
Focus on the mind-muscle connection.
Keep rest periods consistent for metabolic stress.
